title: Chunking, Segmentation, Document Structure, and Identity · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs chunking, segmentation, document structure, and identity from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for structure-aware chunking experiment after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.
Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Rag Stable Chunk Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review RAG engineering team handles case 9491 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to choose structure-aware chunks, bounded overlap, stable identity, and parent-child evidence; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive stable document and chunk identity from source, version, structure, offsets, and content so updates, deletes, caches, and citations reconcile.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Stable Chunk Identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Stable Chunk Identity, keep Rag Stable Chunk Identity connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive stable document and chunk identity from source, version, structure, offsets, and content so updates, deletes, caches, and citations reconcile.

hints: For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Stable Chunk Identity, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Stable Chunk Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Stable Chunk Identity, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review RAG engineering team handles case 9491 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to choose structure-aware chunks, bounded overlap, stable identity, and parent-child evidence; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive stable document and chunk identity from source, version, structure, offsets, and content so updates, deletes, caches, and citations reconcile.

For RAG and Python Record what required effort · Review Rag Stable Chunk Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Random chunk IDs are sufficient for incremental indexing and deletion.
